name: add-imessage
tagline: Adds iMessage channel support to NanoClaw via a Chat SDK adapter, in local macOS or remote Photon API mode.
verdict: Solid integrations skill: pinned dependency, idempotent steps (skip-if-present import, upsert-and-remove env script), and a thorough troubleshooting section. No security red flags in its network, credential, or FDA-permission handling. Weak spots: no 'not for' scoping against sibling add-<channel> skills, pnpm build/test steps run with no declared.

highlights.summary: No 'NOT for'/out-of-scope language anywhere; sibling add-<channel> skills (Slack, WhatsApp, etc. implied by the pattern) could misfire without exclusions.
`pnpm run build` and `pnpm exec vitest run ...` (lines 53, 56) are invoked with no stated pnpm requirement or install check, unlike the explicit Darwin check for local mode.
No version field in frontmatter (lines 1-4) and no changelog or dated revision notes anywhere in the body.
